diagrams.title: Linear Transformation Mapping
diagrams.description: Shows how vectors in domain ℝ² transform to codomain ℝ² under a linear transformation
elements: Lines through origin map to lines through origin
diagrams.purpose: Understand that linear transformations are functions between vector spaces that preserve vector space structure

diagrams.title: Kernel (Null Space) Visualization
diagrams.description: Depicts how the kernel is the set of input vectors that collapse to zero output
elements: Kernel subspace (shown as a line through origin)
Arrows from kernel vectors all point to 0
Kernel dimension + Range dimension = Domain dimension
diagrams.purpose: Visualize kernel as the 'information loss' zone where multiple inputs map to the same (zero) output

diagrams.title: Range (Image) Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows the subset of codomain actually reached by the transformation
elements: Not onto: Range is proper subset
Vectors in range shown as images of domain vectors
diagrams.purpose: Understand onto transformations and the relationship between domain, codomain, and actual outputs

diagrams.title: Rank-Nullity Theorem Diagram
diagrams.description: Illustrates the decomposition of domain into kernel and complementary subspace
elements: Kernel (null space) shown as collapsed portion
Arrow showing transformation maps kernel to 0, rest to range
diagrams.purpose: Visual proof understanding of the fundamental theorem connecting kernel and range dimensions
